name: Lesvos
subtitle: The island of ouzo, Sappho, and petrified forests
intro: Greece's third-largest island is one of its most rewarding. Lesvos combines exceptional natural diversity — volcanic landscapes, pine forests, salt marshes — with a deep cultural heritage stretching from ancient Sappho to the Nobel Prize-winning poet Elytis. It rewards slow travel.

itinerary.title: 6-Day Lesvos Itinerary
itinerary.subtitle: A complete circuit of the island — from the Aegean's ouzo capital to the birthplace of Sappho, medieval castles to petrified forests.

stops.name: Mytilene Town
stops.desc: Arrive and settle in. Walk the waterfront promenade and explore the old harbour.

stops.name: Mytilene Castle
stops.desc: One of the largest medieval castles in the Aegean, with panoramic harbour views.

stops.name: Varia Village
stops.desc: Home to the Museum of Theophilos and Tériade Museum — two world-class art collections.

stops.name: Agios Isidoros Beach
stops.desc: Relaxed sandy beach on the south coast, sheltered and calm. Evening swim.

stops.name: Mytilene
stops.desc: Depart south after breakfast via the mountain road.

stops.name: Agiasos
stops.desc: Charming mountain village with cobbled lanes and a lively carnival tradition.

stops.name: Plomari
stops.desc: Ouzo capital of Greece. Tour the Barbayiannis distillery and sample the local product.

stops.name: Melinda Beach
stops.desc: Secluded fine pebble cove west of Plomari, tucked under a pine-clad hillside.

stops.name: Vatera Beach
stops.desc: 8 km of uninterrupted golden sand — one of the longest beaches in the Aegean. Stay overnight.

stops.name: Vatera
stops.desc: Depart west along the southern coastal road.

stops.name: Petrified Forest
stops.desc: UNESCO-listed Petrified Forest — 20 million year old trees turned to stone by volcanic ash.

stops.name: Sigri
stops.desc: Westernmost village of Lesvos. Tiny whitewashed harbour and the Natural History Museum.

stops.name: Faneromeni Beach
stops.desc: Wild, exposed beach north of Sigri with dramatic sea-carved cliffs and vivid turquoise water.

stops.name: Skala Eresou
stops.desc: Birthplace of Sappho. Laid-back beach village with a long sandy shore. Stay the night.

stops.name: Skala Eresou
stops.desc: Morning coffee on the beach before heading north.

stops.name: Kalloni
stops.desc: Main inland market town. The Gulf of Kalloni sardines are famous across Greece.

stops.name: Skala Kalloni
stops.desc: Fishing port on the sheltered Gulf of Kalloni, a world-class birdwatching destination.

stops.name: Parakila
stops.desc: Quiet village on the northern shore of the gulf.

stops.name: Molyvos (Mithymna)
stops.desc: Most photogenic village in Lesvos — a Byzantine castle perched above a cobbled harbour. Stay here.

stops.name: Molyvos Castle
stops.desc: Morning walk up to the Byzantine castle. On clear days you can see the Turkish coast.

stops.name: Eftalou Hot Springs
stops.desc: Ottoman-era thermal baths built directly on the beach. The domed stone bathhouse dates to the 19th century.

stops.name: Petra
stops.desc: Village dominated by a 33-metre monolithic rock with the Church of the Sweet-Kissing Virgin on top.

stops.name: Anaxos Beach
stops.desc: Long sandy beach facing southwest, with clear views of Molyvos castle. Afternoon swim.

stops.name: Lepetymnos Summit
stops.desc: The highest peak of northern Lesvos at 968 m. Pine forests, views of both coastlines.

stops.name: Mandamados
stops.desc: Famous for the icon of Archangel Michael moulded from soil and monks' blood, and for its pottery.

stops.name: Tsonia Beach
stops.desc: Remote northern beach accessible by a dirt road — pristine, empty, and wild.

stops.name: Thermi
stops.desc: Spa village with natural thermal springs 12 km north of Mytilene, used since antiquity.

stops.name: Mytilene Town
stops.desc: Return to Mytilene. Final lunch at the harbour. Depart by ferry or flight.

beaches.name: Skala Eresou Beach
beaches.facilities: Moderate: beach bars, tavernas, some sunbeds. Village is directly behind the beach.
beaches.desc: The top beach on Lesvos. A long dark-sand beach in front of the village of Skala Eresou, birthplace of the poet Sappho. Relaxed, bohemian character and far less touristy than the Cyclades. Warm, calm water in summer.

beaches.name: Agios Isidoros Beach
beaches.depth: Shallow — gently shelving, safe for children
beaches.facing: South-facing — well sheltered from north winds
beaches.facilities: Good: sunbeds, umbrellas, tavernas, parking nearby
beaches.desc: A fine sandy beach on the south coast, sheltered and consistently calm. Popular with locals but never overcrowded. Clear, warm water and easy access make it one of the best all-round choices on the island.

beaches.name: Vatera Beach
beaches.depth: Shallow to medium — gradually deepening, safe for children
beaches.facing: South-facing — sheltered from the prevailing north winds (meltemi)
beaches.facilities: Well-developed: tavernas, sunbeds & umbrellas, showers, parking, accommodation nearby
beaches.desc: The longest beach on Lesvos — 8 uninterrupted kilometres of golden fine sand. Exceptionally calm water. The best choice for families or those wanting full facilities and space.

beaches.name: Anaxos Beach
beaches.depth: Shallow — very gradual slope, excellent for children
beaches.facing: Southwest-facing — protected from north and east winds
beaches.facilities: Well-equipped: sunbeds, umbrellas, beach bars, tavernas, showers, small shops
beaches.desc: A long fine sandy beach 5 km south of Petra with stunning views of Molyvos castle. Shallow, calm water and good facilities. One of the most consistently calm beaches on the island.

beaches.name: Petra Beach
beaches.depth: Medium depth — clear water, gradually deepening
beaches.facing: West-facing — calm in the mornings, some afternoon breeze
beaches.facilities: Good: sunbeds, beach bars, tavernas a short walk away in the village
beaches.desc: Set directly in front of the village of Petra, dominated by the extraordinary 33-metre rock with a chapel on top. Crystal clear water and easy access to one of Lesvos' most striking villages.
